message:
refactor(runtime): deduplicate blocking pool spawn and add sender-drop safety

BlockingPool: Extract the duplicated spawn_with_priority logic from both
BlockingPool and BlockingPoolHandle into a shared spawn_task_on_inner()
free function. Both types now delegate to this single implementation,
eliminating ~80 lines of duplicated task creation, shutdown-check, TOCTOU
closure, and notification code. Remove now-unused maybe_spawn_thread and
notify_one methods from BlockingPool.

BlockingOneshot: Add Drop impl on the sender side that marks the channel
as done with closed_without_result=true if the sender is dropped without
calling send(). The receiver's Future impl distinguishes between a normal
result, a double-consume panic, and a sender-drop assertion with a clear
"blocking operation ended without producing a result" message. New test
blocking_oneshot_sender_drop_fails_closed verifies the panic.

New test helper wait_for_pending_count() with 1s timeout for reliable
synchronization in handle_spawn_accepted_before_shutdown_still_runs test,
which verifies that tasks enqueued via a handle before shutdown are not
retroactively cancelled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
